title: Quickfix and Location Lists
subtitle: A typed list of file:line:message — the spine of grep, make, and lint workflows.
summary: :make, :grep, and :vimgrep populate the quickfix list. :copen shows it; :cnext / :cprev step through entries. The location list is the per-window twin.

blocks.text: The quickfix list is Vim's universal results pane. Compile errors, grep hits, lint warnings, test failures — they all funnel into the same list with the same navigation keys. Once you wire your tools through it, every command-line workflow becomes a structured editing session.

rows: Pass arguments to the make program
Run external grep (`'grepprg'`), populate from results
Recursively find TODO in Python files

sequence.note: Open the quickfix window (fills the bottom)

blocks.text: In the quickfix window, {key:Enter} opens the entry under the cursor. {key:p} previews it without leaving. {key::}{key:c}{key:o}{key:l}{key:d}{key:e}{key:r} restores a previous quickfix list — Vim keeps a stack of them.
Location list — same idea, scoped to a window

Replace every {key:c}-prefixed command with {key:l}: {key::}{key:l}{key:m}{key:a}{key:k}{key:e}, {key::}{key:l}{key:g}{key:r}{key:e}{key:p}, {key::}{key:l}{key:o}{key:p}{key:e}{key:n}, {key::}{key:l}{key:n}{key:e}{key:x}{key:t}, {key::}{key:l}{key:p}{key:r}{key:e}{key:v}. The list lives on the window, not the editor, so two splits can run two greps without stepping on each other.

blocks.title: Quickfix is a list of typed entries
blocks.text: Internally each quickfix entry is `{ filename, lnum, col, type, text, valid }`. Population functions (`:make`, `:grep`, `:cexpr`) convert tool output into that struct via the `'errorformat'` scanf-like parser. The quickfix window is a buffer rendered from that list — that's why you can't really edit it (the renderer overwrites your changes). Navigation commands index into the list, open the entry's file (in the current window), and place the cursor at `(lnum, col)`.

blocks.title: Location lists are per-window quickfix
blocks.text: Vim keeps two parallel data structures: one global quickfix list (shared by all windows) and one location list per window. They use identical entry structure, identical commands modulo prefix, identical errorformat parsing. The duplication is intentional: it lets you run a project-wide search in the quickfix list while simultaneously running a file-local search in the location list of one window, without overwrites. The window-tied lifetime also means closing the window discards its location list.

Set {key::}{key:s}{key:e}{key:t} {key:g}{key:r}{key:e}{key:p}{key:p}{key:r}{key:g}{key:=}{key:r}{key:g} {key:--vimgrep} to use ripgrep — it's massively faster than grep and produces output matching Vim's default `'grepformat'`.
